name: Black Triangle Sightings
description: Black triangle sightings refer to reports of large, silent, triangular-shaped aircraft, often observed at low altitudes, with bright lights at each corner. These sightings became prevalent in the late 20th century and continue to the present day, primarily in the United States, the United Kingdom, and Belgium. Proponents suggest these craft are either advanced, top-secret military aircraft of unknown origin, or extraterrestrial vehicles. Key evidence cited includes eyewitness accounts, photographs, and videos of the alleged craft. Some sightings coincide with known military exercises, fueling speculation of experimental aircraft. Mainstream perspectives attribute these sightings to misidentification of conventional aircraft, such as formations of planes or helicopters, atmospheric phenomena, or hoaxes. Skeptics point to the lack of verifiable physical evidence, such as radar confirmation or debris. The lack of consistent descriptions across sightings also raises doubts about a single, unified phenomenon. Psychological factors, such as suggestibility and pareidolia (seeing patterns in random stimuli), are also cited as potential explanations for some reports. The black triangle phenomenon remains a contested topic, with proponents maintaining the reality of these unidentified aerial objects and skeptics emphasizing the lack of credible evidence.
subtitle: Unexplained triangular aircraft sightings.

claims.claim: Black triangles are advanced, secret military aircraft.
claims.evidence: Sightings often occur near military bases and coincide with reported military exercises.
claims.criticism: No official acknowledgement of such aircraft exists; known advanced military aircraft designs do not match reported characteristics of black triangles. Sightings near military bases may simply be due to higher air traffic.

claims.evidence: The craft exhibit unusual flight characteristics, such as hovering silently and making sharp turns at high speeds, exceeding known aircraft capabilities.
claims.criticism: Eyewitness accounts are often unreliable and subject to misinterpretation. The reported flight characteristics could be exaggerated or misunderstood. Lack of physical evidence to support extraterrestrial origin.

claims.claim: Black triangles are a single, unified phenomenon.
claims.evidence: Consistent descriptions of a large, silent, triangular aircraft with bright lights at each corner across multiple independent sightings.
claims.criticism: Significant variations exist in reported details, such as size, light configuration, and flight patterns, suggesting multiple explanations or misidentification of various objects. Witness suggestibility and pareidolia may contribute to perceived consistency.

keyFigures.name: Nick Pope
keyFigures.role: Former UK Ministry of Defence UFO investigator

mythologyConnections: Vimana (ancient flying machines in Hindu mythology)

timeline.event: Initial reports of black triangle sightings in the Hudson Valley, New York.
Belgian UFO wave, including numerous black triangle sightings.
Continued reports of black triangle sightings globally, documented online and in UFO research communities.

sources.author: David Marler
sources.work: Triangular UFOs: An Estimate of the Situation
sources.title: David Marler
